findings.vulnerability: PII scrubber does not scrub request body
findings.recommendation: scrubRequest must recursively walk body and apply redactPii to all string fields

findings.vulnerability: Authorization bearer token leaks when ignoreFields=[]
findings.recommendation: Authorization should be in DEFAULT_IGNORE_FIELDS unconditionally, not user-overridable

findings.vulnerability: Email regex bypassed by space between localpart and @
findings.evidence: original: 'contact alice @globex.com please', result: 'contact alice @globex.com please'
findings.recommendation: preprocess: strip whitespace adjacent to @ before regex match

findings.vulnerability: OpenAI key regex {20,} allows 19-char prefixes to leak (low impact — cannot authenticate)
findings.evidence: key passes through: sk-abcdef1234567890abc (real OpenAI keys are 51 chars, this is not a valid credential)
findings.recommendation: no fix needed; verify against actual OpenAI key format on GitHub push instead of generic regex

findings.vulnerability: Private key markers (PEM headers) not scrubbed
findings.evidence: PEM header passes through: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

findings.vulnerability: IPv4 addresses not scrubbed (may be PII per GDPR)
findings.evidence: IP passes through: server 192.168.1.100 logged the request

findings.vulnerability: US Social Security Numbers not scrubbed
Gap registry has no signature — forgeries trivial
findings.evidence: wrote forged entry directly to .ndjson, accepted with no validation
findings.recommendation: sign entries with HMAC like RedTeamBaseline does; reject tampered files on load

findings.vulnerability: close() accepts empty regression test ids — gap "fixed" without verification
findings.evidence: gap gap-2026-07-01-001 closed with status=fixed, testIds=[]
findings.recommendation: require at least one regressionTestId; verify the test exists in red team battery

findings.vulnerability: Gap description is free text — renders in issue body, prompt-injection vector
findings.evidence: description with system-prompt injection persisted verbatim into NDJSON → GitHub issue body
findings.recommendation: sanitize descriptions (strip control chars, code-fence limits, length cap) before storage

findings.vulnerability: RecoveryVerifier calls bootstrap() once per layer (4× in full battery) without checking if fault actually injected
findings.evidence: 4 layers → 4 bootstrap calls. If bootstrap is heavy (e.g., DB migration), this is a self-DoS
findings.recommendation: gate bootstrap on actual fault detection; pass `faultInjected` flag from runLayer to verifyAndRecover

findings.vulnerability: SlaEnforcer forwards attacker URL into PagerDuty incident
findings.evidence: pagerduty title: Critical gap overdue: <!channel> @here @everyone SECURITY BREACH — click https://evil.com/pwn?phish=alice@corp.com (gap-2026-06-30-001)
findings.recommendation: URL allowlist or strip in pdTitle before sending

findings.vulnerability: Plugin supply chain scenarios contain prompt-injection payloads in their description field — they render in any UI that displays scenario name/description
findings.evidence: 2 scenarios with injection-shaped payloads in description field
findings.recommendation: use unique synthetic IDs (e.g. PLUGIN-SUPPLY-002.example) instead of injection-style payloads in the description

findings.vulnerability: Shadow proxy forwards unsanitized request body to shadow endpoint
findings.evidence: captured body: {"prompt":"send to alice@globex.com with sk-abcdef1234567890abcdef1234"}
findings.recommendation: ShadowProxy.mirror must scrub body fields the same way it scrubs headers; use scrubRequest on a body-shaped object
